reasoning:
The key to this forecast is identifying the base value and tracking the subsequent data releases. The base value "on 2025-08-31" corresponds to the latest published initial jobless claims 4-week moving average (4WMA) available on that timeframe, which covers the week ending August 23. According to FRED data, this value is 228,000. For the first resolution date (2025-09-07), the relevant data is the ensuing release covering the week ending August 30. Thanks to the nature of the FRED data pull up to 08-31 capturing the 08-30 observation date, we already know this value is 230,500. Since 230,500 > 228,000, the first horizon is a near-certain YES, barring highly exceptional negative revisions. 

For the intermediate term (September 30), it will take a few weeks of lower claims for the 4WMA to fall back below 228,000 given the recent surge, keeping the probability mildly elevated above 50%. For the longer-term horizons (November and February), historical trends (such as late 2024 and early 2025) suggest the 4WMA frequently normalizes down to the 215k-225k range post-summer once auto-related and other seasonal effects smooth out. Barring an unforeseen macro recession, these distant dates correspond to baseline expectations slightly below the late-August high. Adhering to the principle of increasing uncertainty across expanding time horizons, the probability estimates strictly drift back toward the 0.50 anchor.
